apply: Students will be able to apply the principles of federalism to real-world scenarios, such as resolving conflicts between the central and state governments, or evaluating the effectiveness of a federal system.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of federalism on social and economic policies, such as healthcare, education, and infrastructure development.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the strengths and weaknesses of different federal systems, such as the United States, Germany, and India, and evaluate their effectiveness in addressing the needs of diverse populations.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of federalism on regional and local identities, and evaluate the role of federalism in promoting national integration.
remember: Students will be able to recall key terms and concepts related to federalism, such as the meaning and types of federalism, merits and demerits of federalism, etc.
Students will be able to identify and explain the different levels of government in a federal system, such as central government, state government, and local government.
understand: Students will be able to explain the principles of federalism, including the division of power between the central and state governments, and the concept of autonomy and representation.
Students will be able to understand the role of the judiciary in a federal system and its relationship with the legislative and executive branches.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT, the objectives of this chapter are to understand the concept of federalism, its types, and its characteristics.
To analyze the advantages and disadvantages of federalism and understand its relevance in the Indian context.
To evaluate the role of the central and state governments in a federal system and understand the relationship between the judiciary, legislature, and executive branches.
board_exam_objectives: To test the student's understanding of the concept of federalism and its types.
To evaluate the student's ability to identify and explain the strengths and weaknesses of federalism.
To assess the student's ability to apply the principles of federalism to real-world scenarios and evaluate its impact on social and economic policies.
